seoTitle: Post and Release Quiz Scores in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Finish grading and officially release quiz scores to students in Microsoft Forms using the Post Scores dashboard, individually or in bulk.
intro: Microsoft Forms keeps quiz scores hidden while you grade to protect academic integrity, so releasing results depends on activating a separate Post Scores step once grading is complete. This guide walks through reviewing submissions, finishing manual grading, and pushing the final scores out to your class.

steps.heading: Open the Responses tab
steps.body: Go to your quiz's Responses tab to see the full list of student submissions.

steps.heading: Review and grade answers
steps.body: Click Review Answers to audit individual student papers, adjust point values, and add written feedback where needed.

steps.heading: Return to the Responses screen
steps.body: Click Back once manual grading is finished for the students you want to release scores to.

steps.heading: Open Post Scores
steps.body: Select the Post Scores option to view your class roster and see which profiles are marked as Evaluated.

steps.heading: Release scores in bulk or individually
steps.body: Check the master selection box to release to your entire class at once, or select individual students, then click the Post Scores confirmation button.

faq.question: Can students see their scores before I post them?
faq.answer: No, scores stay hidden from students until you actively use the Post Scores dashboard to release them.

faq.question: How do students view their released scores?
faq.answer: Students click their original quiz invitation link to load their personal scorecard once scores are posted.

faq.question: Can I release scores to just one student instead of the whole class?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select individual student rows instead of using the master select-all option.

faq.question: Can I add written feedback along with the score?
faq.answer: Yes, the Review Answers screen lets you type personalized feedback for each student alongside their point adjustments.
